title: THE SIX — LIFELINE CUT V5

events.text: If we're going to survive, you need to know who we were.
Not as a list.
Lists are what the station keeps after it has already failed the people on them.
I'm leaving the damaged relay console and heading toward the lower access spine.
Life support has an old personnel index nearby. If someone was erased, the systems around them may still remember the shape of the hole.
I need both hands to secure the portable relay. If I drop it, you lose me.

directorNotes.objective: Guide Maya from the damaged relay console toward life-support records and expose the erased seventh crew member.

events.text: Cruel question.
Fair question.
Grant handled security. He called himself Head of Not Dying.
Annoying title. Worse part is, he was usually right.
Rivera solved problems before you finished explaining them. It made you grateful and furious at the same time.
Park joked when he was scared. Owens pretended not to laugh, then fixed his coffee ratio when he wasn't looking.
Hale pretended he didn't need anyone.
Worst liar on the station.
That's what hurts. ECHO didn't erase strangers.
It erased people with bad jokes, favorite mugs, unfinished arguments.

event.text: Left turn ahead. Deck plating is bowed here. I'm stepping around it.
event.speaker: MAYA

events.title: CREW MANIFEST
events.content:
01 Ethan Hale
02 Maya Chen
03 Grant Walker
04 Rivera Torres
05 Owen Park
06 Sarah Owens
events.caption: Recovered official ECHO crew manifest.

events.text: Six names.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: That's the official story.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Official stories always sound confident.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Right up until they start bleeding.
events.speaker: MAYA

choices.text: SEND MAYA TO LIFE SUPPORT INDEX.

events.text: Hale kept the station breathing.
Power. Relay routing. Pressure control. Emergency doors.
If something opened or closed, Hale either had a way into it or a lecture about why you shouldn't touch it.
He corrected reports in red. Digital reports. Red comments. In space.
I told him that was hostile.
He said accuracy wasn't a friendship contest.
The real Hale was hard to like sometimes.
But he was ours.
I'm at the lower access spine.
No lights. Just emergency strips and that thin little hum oxygen systems make when they're trying not to die.
There are two ways to life support from here.
Main corridor is faster. Maintenance crawl is quieter.
Fast saves air. Quiet saves attention. I hate that those are separate things.
Main corridor.
Moving fast. Keeping the relay close to my chest.
If I stop answering, assume I'm trying not to breathe too loudly.
Maintenance crawl.
Slower. Quieter.
Everything is too close to my face and every cable feels like a hand.
I'm going in. Radio clipped high. One hand on the relay pack.
Life-support hatch is ahead.
Panel is half dead.
Manual latch still works, because Hale designed things for disasters and then pretended that wasn't paranoia.
I can force it open now, or run a pressure scan first.
Force is faster. Scan is smarter. I'm tired enough to resent both.
Forcing it.
Left hand on the latch. Right hand bracing the frame.
This is going to be loud.
Scanning first.
I'm setting the reader against the seam. The panel is cold except around the latch.
That means pressure is trapped behind it.

events.title: LIFE SUPPORT ALLOCATION
events.content:
ECHO LIFE SUPPORT SNAPSHOT

Assigned crew capacity: 6
Active biometric loads before blackout: 7
Oxygen adjustment event: automatic
Unauthorized load marker: removed from index
events.caption: Recovered life support allocation record. Crew count mismatch detected.
events.message: [LIFE SUPPORT ALLOCATION ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: Seven biometric loads.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Life support doesn't care about names.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: It counts bodies.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: And before blackout, it counted one more than the manifest.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: That means Number Seven was not just paperwork. They breathed this air.
events.speaker: MAYA

choices.text: ASK IF SHE KNEW ABOUT SEVEN.

events.text: No.
And I know how convenient that sounds.
I don't remember a bunk. I don't remember another voice. I don't remember another ration request.
But now that I know there should be a gap...
I can feel one.
Like a room in my head with the door painted over.
That's the part I hate.
EVA storage is two compartments over.
I can cut through suit prep, or go around through decon.
Suit prep is direct. Decon is safer but costs oxygen.
Suit prep also means walking past the racks. I know what is probably still hanging there.
I am pretending that does not bother me. It does.
Suit prep.
Direct route. Unstable debris. Bad memories hanging on hooks.
I'm crossing quickly. If I start describing every suit, I won't keep moving.
Decon route.
Safer. Longer. Oxygen tax included.
I'm going through. The lights are still cycling, so at least this room remembers its job.

events.title: EVA SUIT REGISTRY
events.content:
ECHO EVA SUIT REGISTRY

SUIT-01: ETHAN HALE
SUIT-02: MAYA CHEN
SUIT-03: GRANT WALKER
SUIT-04: RIVERA TORRES
SUIT-05: OWEN PARK
SUIT-06: SARAH OWENS
SUIT-07: ENTRY ERASED

Registry repair failed.
events.caption: Recovered EVA suit registry. Seventh suit slot exists but identity is erased.
events.message: [EVA SUIT REGISTRY ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: Seven suit slots.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Six names. Seven bodies. Seven suits.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: No puzzle needed. Even I can hate that math.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.title: MANIFEST_V2
events.content:
01 Ethan Hale
02 Maya Chen
03 Grant Walker
04 Rivera Torres
05 Owen Park
06 Sarah Owens
07 █████████

Layer status: identity erased, not redacted.
events.caption: Recovered alternate manifest layer. Seventh entry erased.

events.text: Entry erased. Not redacted.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Redacted means someone wanted to hide a name.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Erased means someone wanted the place where the name lived to stop existing.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: There it is.
Seven lines.
Six people allowed to remain people.
One turned into a wound in the file.
I don't know who Number Seven is.
No. That's wrong.
The name existed.
Someone made existing the problem.
And now the station is angry that we noticed.

choices.text: ASK WHERE YOU SAW THE NAME.

events.text: There it is.
One whisper from the dark and suddenly I'm the suspect.
I get it. When you're scared, a person is easier to blame than a hole in reality.
Fine. Keep that question.
Just don't let it stop you from asking the right one.
Because whoever erased Number Seven did not stop at a name.
They changed the way the station remembers.

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: FIRST CONTACT

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: FIRST FILE

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: FIRST LIE

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: NOT HALE

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: BEFORE HALE

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: ETHAN HALE WAS NOT THE FIRST VOICE.
Operator...
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Ask the archive what spoke before Hale.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: And if I go quiet after this, don't assume I'm angry.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Assume I'm moving.
events.speaker: MAYA

choices.text: ASK MAYA ABOUT THE FIRST VOICE.

e05_r01_relay_secured.speaker: MAYA
e05_r01_relay_secured.objective: Maya returns after securing the portable relay.

events.text: I'm mobile.
Relay pack is strapped across my back. It is ugly, hot, and vibrating against my spine.
If it starts judging my posture, I'm cutting it loose.
The access spine is ahead. Emergency strips only. Everything else is dark.
You can pull the official manifest while I move, or you can ask me who they were before the station turned them into entries.

choices.text: PULL THE OFFICIAL MANIFEST WHILE SHE MOVES.
ASK MAYA WHO THE CREW REALLY WERE.

e05_r04_main_return.speaker: MAYA
e05_r04_main_return.objective: Maya returns after crossing the main corridor.

events.text: I'm at the life-support hatch.
Something shifted behind me while I crossed.
Not chasing.
Not yet.
I had to move fast. Oxygen dropped harder than I wanted.

e05_r04_maintenance_return.speaker: MAYA
e05_r04_maintenance_return.objective: Maya returns after moving through the maintenance crawl.

events.text: I'm through.
Quiet route worked. I think.
Found something wedged in the crawlspace.

events.title: GRANT'S SECURITY TAG
events.content:
DAMAGED ID TAG
NAME: GRANT WALKER
ACCESS: SECURITY / LOWER SPINE
STATUS: OFFLINE
NOTE: Impact scoring along edge.
events.caption: Maya recovered Grant Walker's damaged security tag in the maintenance crawl.
events.message: [GRANT'S SECURITY TAG ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: Grant would've hated that I found him like this.
He liked exits clearly marked and threats politely announced.
Life-support hatch is ahead.

e05_r06_force_return.speaker: MAYA
e05_r06_force_return.objective: Maya returns after forcing the life-support hatch.

events.text: Open.
Loud. Very loud.
Pressure coughed through the seam and slapped my visor hard enough to blur the glass.
I'm fine.
Mostly fine. My hand is shaking. Don't make a big thing out of it.

e05_r06_scan_return.speaker: MAYA
e05_r06_scan_return.objective: Maya returns after scanning and venting the hatch.

events.text: Good call.
There was a pressure pocket behind the hatch. Small, but enough to ruin my day.
I vented it slowly through the service seam.
I hate how grateful I am for caution.
Hatch is open now.

e05_r09_suit_prep_return.speaker: MAYA
e05_r09_suit_prep_return.objective: Maya returns after crossing suit prep.

events.text: I'm through.
I kicked something.
Helmet.
Empty. Cracked visor.
I'm not looking inside it again.
EVA storage access is in front of me.

e05_r09_decon_return.speaker: MAYA
e05_r09_decon_return.objective: Maya returns after taking the decon route.

events.text: I'm through decon.
Lights still cycle. That's almost comforting.
Almost. It smells like metal and old antiseptic.
I used to hate that smell. Right now it means the room still remembers how to be a room.
EVA storage access is ahead.

upgradeFocus: Same story spine preserved from Operation Cut V2.
Maya's movement and physical tasks are now real-time characterBusy events.
Return nodes report concrete things Maya saw, heard, felt, and did.
Crew exposition is framed as Maya's memory rather than static lore.
Seven-person contradiction remains the core mystery.
All decision points retain exactly two choices.
Effects are corrected into effects fields instead of malformed nextEpisodeId objects.
directorCut.summary: Maya travels from the lower access spine to life-support and EVA storage, exposing the contradiction between six official crew names and seven biological/suit records. The episode ends by redirecting the mystery toward the first contact record and the first voice before Hale.

episodeObjective: Guide Maya from the damaged relay console to life-support and EVA storage, prove the six-name/seven-body contradiction, and end with the first-contact/first-voice cliffhanger.

summary: Scenario spine preserved: official manifest, life-support mismatch, EVA suit registry, erased seventh entry, first voice clue.
Maya physically moves through the station and disappears during real tasks using characterBusy events.
Return nodes report what happened while Maya was away.
ProgressTask movement sections replaced with characterBusy + returnNodeId.
Every visible choice group has exactly two choices.
Dialogue rewritten to feel human, specific, and operational.
Lore is delivered through lived memory, environment, and recoverable station evidence.
